alerts.title: Israel escalates strikes on Beirut amid US-Iran deal, Iran warns of derailment
alerts.summary: Israeli forces launched multiple strikes on Beirut's southern suburbs despite Trump criticism, killing at least 3 civilians, per NBC News, Reuters, and Al Jazeera (2026-06-18 incidents feed). Iran formally warned that continued Israeli military action could derail the nascent US-Iran MoU. Trump publicly condemned the strikes as counterproductive, creating acute diplomatic tension between US commitment to the deal and Israel's operational independence in Lebanon theater.

alerts.title: Iran-linked group claims FBI drone hack; Pentagon AI system deployed against Iran
alerts.summary: Iran-linked threat actors claim successful compromise of FBI drone systems and threat World Cup targets, per France 24 and CBS News (2026-06-18 global_metrics cyber headlines). Separately, Rybar Telegram reports Pentagon officially acknowledged use of Elon Musk's Grok Gov AI model via Maven Smart System in operation against Iran, enabling 2000+ rocket strikes on 2000 separate targets in 96 hours (2026-06-17). Dual cyber and AI-enabled kinetic escalation marks unprecedented integration of commercial AI into active warfighting.

alerts.title: Houthis declare total ban on Israeli shipping; Red Sea escalation threatens global commerce
alerts.summary: Yemen's Houthis announced comprehensive ban on all Israeli vessels transiting the Red Sea and resumed attacks on Israel, per The Long War Journal and Fox News (2026-06-18 incidents feed). Military expert warned of imminent 'general escalation' if Houthi campaign fully resumes. Five US Navy vessels currently deployed in Red Sea (USS Eisenhower, USS Bataan, USS Carney, USS Mason, USS Laboon) face heightened collision and engagement risk during proxy intensification.

alerts.title: US domestic sentiment turns negative on Iran deal; 'disaster' rhetoric dominates discourse
alerts.summary: US sentiment analysis shows 46% negative vs 44% positive on Iran policy (2026-06-18), reflecting deterioration since prior cycle. Top keywords reveal sustained spike in 'war' (14 mentions), 'deal' (31 mentions), and negative framing alongside Trump (71 mentions) dominance. Absence of 'Versailles' spike from prior alert suggests refocused but grimly sustained skepticism of MoU framework, particularly amid simultaneous Israeli strikes and revealed US cyber-kinetic ops against Iran.

alerts.title: US-Iran nuclear MoU leaves 60% enriched uranium in-country pending dilution agreement
alerts.summary: Leaked text of US-Iran MoU reveals plan to dilute Iran's 60% U-235 enriched stockpile rather than remove it, per Jerusalem Post and Reuters (2026-06-18 global_metrics nuclear headlines). Breakout timeline remains ~1-2 weeks. Multiple expert analyses flag disposal mechanism uncertainty and Kazakhstan's potential pivotal role in uranium handling. Deal credibility hinges on verification of dilution execution while Iran maintains technical capability for rapid weaponization.

analysis_note: The US-Iran MoU faces acute stress from concurrent Israeli escalation in Lebanon, revealed Pentagon AI-enabled kinetic ops, Iranian cyber claims against US assets, Houthi shipping bans, and persistent domestic US skepticism—creating multi-front pressure on deal durability and increasing collision risk across Red Sea, Persian Gulf, and Israel-Lebanon theaters.
